sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.18 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Suppose that$G$is a finite group, and$A_1, A_2, A_3$are subsets of$G$such that the multiplication map$A_1 A_2 A_3 G$is bijective. Must the subgroup$A_2$generated by$A_2$have order divisible by the cardinality$|A_2|$? This is Question~8 in (G.\,M.\,Bergman, J.~Iranian Math. Soc., { 1} (2020), 157--161). It is known ( that the corresponding statement is true for the subgroups$A_1$and$A_3$. Moreover,...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
